join_order.notes: Scan sub 86K rows, filter sic [4000,4999] → ~4.3K qualifying rows. Build tiny qualifying_adsh_set (hash set, cap=next_power_of_2(4307*2)=8192). Also build qualifying_adsh_to_sub_row map (adsh_code -> {sic, cik}) for O(1) value retrieval. This set is L1-resident and eliminates ~95% of num rows before expensive index probes.

mmap indexes/sub_adsh_hash.bin (2MB). Layout: [uint32_t cap=262144][{int32_t adsh_code, uint32_t row_idx} x 262144]. Hash: (adsh_code*2654435761u)&(cap-1). Linear probe bounded (C24). Retrieve sub_sic[row_idx], sub_cik[row_idx] for qualifying adsh_codes.

mmap indexes/tag_tv_hash.bin (48MB, fits L3=44MB with warm access). Layout: [uint32_t cap=4194304][{int32_t tag_code, int32_t ver_code, uint32_t row_idx} x 4194304]. Empty sentinel: tag_code==INT32_MIN (use std::fill NOT memset, C20). Hash: ((tag_code*2654435761u)^(ver_code*1234567891u))&(cap-1). Check tag_abstract[row_idx]==0; retrieve tag_tlabel[row_idx].

mmap indexes/pre_eq_hash.bin (48MB). Layout: [uint32_t cap=4194304][{int32_t adsh, int32_t tag, int32_t ver} x 4194304]. Empty sentinel: adsh_code==INT32_MIN. Hash: ((a*2654435761u)^(b*1234567891u)^(c*2246822519u))&(cap-1). Existence check only — emit row only if (num.adsh, num.tag, num.version) found. Bounded probe (C24).

filters.implementation: int16_t usd_code = find_code(uom_dict, 'USD'); filter uom[i] == usd_code
check for NaN or sentinel; apply in same pass as uom filter
direct int32_t range check: sic[i] >= 4000 && sic[i] <= 4999
handled entirely by pre_eq_hash pre-built index (only EQ rows indexed)

data_structures.qualifying_adsh_set: compact hash_set<int32_t>, cap=next_power_of_2(4307*2)=8192, ~32KB, L1-resident; used for fast pre-filter before sub_adsh_hash probe
data_structures.pre_eq_existence: pre_built_index (mmap indexes/pre_eq_hash.bin, 48MB); existence check only, zero build cost (P11)
data_structures.aggregation_map: thread_local open_addressing hash_map, key=packed uint64_t((uint32_t)sic<<32|(uint32_t)tlabel_code), value={int64_t sum_cents, int64_t count, unordered_set<int32_t> distinct_ciks}; cap=next_power_of_2(10000*2)=32768 per thread (sized to full estimated group cardinality, not groups/nthreads, C9); ~few thousand active groups per thread

parallelism.notes: Parallel scan over num rows (39.4M) in 65536-row morsels. Each thread owns a thread-local aggregation map. After scan barrier, sequential merge of 64 thread-local maps into global result (T*G ~64*5K=320K entries total — merge is fast given tiny per-thread maps). P17/P20: NEVER use shared aggregation map during scan.

indexes.purpose: semi_join existence: (adsh_code, tag_code, ver_code) → bool (stmt=EQ check)

aggregation_key.note: p.stmt always 'EQ' due to pre_eq_hash filter — include in output but not in key; C15: key must include ALL GROUP BY dimensions

aggregation_value.sum_cents: int64_t — C29 MANDATORY: sum_cents += llround(value * 100.0); individual values fit double exact range
aggregation_value.count: int64_t — denominator for AVG: avg = (double)sum_cents / 100.0 / count
aggregation_value.distinct_ciks: unordered_set<int32_t> per group — COUNT(DISTINCT cik); at most ~4.3K qualifying CIKs total; union across thread-local sets during merge

output.sort_strategy: std::partial_sort on result vector (O(n log k), k=500)
decode: tlabel_code → tlabel_dict[tlabel_code] (load tag/tlabel_dict.txt at runtime, C2)
stmt → decode stmt_dict[eq_code] = 'EQ' (load pre/stmt_dict.txt at runtime, C2)
usd_code → find_code(uom_dict, 'USD') (load num/uom_dict.txt at runtime, C2)

format.num_companies: size of distinct_ciks set after merge

correctness_notes: C2: Load uom_dict.txt, tlabel_dict.txt, stmt_dict.txt at runtime — never hardcode codes
C9: Thread-local AGG_CAP = next_power_of_2(estimated_groups * 2), sized for FULL group cardinality not groups/nthreads
C15: Aggregation key must pack BOTH sic AND tlabel_code — omitting either collapses groups
C20: Initialize tag_tv_hash and pre_eq_hash sentinel slots with std::fill(..., INT32_MIN), NEVER memset
C24: All index probes use bounded for-loop: for(uint32_t p=0; p<cap; p++) — never unbounded while
C29: SUM(n.value) accumulates as int64_t cents via llround(value*100.0) — reject any double/Kahan accumulator
P11: All three joins use pre-built mmap indexes — zero build cost
P17/P20: Thread-local aggregation mandatory — no shared map during parallel scan
